title: T-094 — FG canonical terminology compatibility pass (UI/i18n/docs, no risky DB/route mass rename)
prompt:
# T-094 — FG canonical terminology compatibility pass

PRD: docs/prd/01-NPD-PRD.md 2026-05-03 FINAL Amendment + _meta/decisions/2026-05-03-flow-d365-settings-technical-decisions.md
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Final decisions this task must implement
- `FG` / Finished Good is the canonical user-facing and domain term.
- `FA` / Factory Article is compatibility alias only.
- Do not perform a risky broad DB/route/event/code mass rename in this task. Existing `fa_code`, `/npd/fa/...`, `fa.*` events or compatibility views may remain if required for stability.
- NPD Builder creates WIP/intermediates + FG + initial shared BOM/product-spec version. D365 is integration/export only.

## Goal
Make the product surface consistently present `FG` terminology while preserving backwards-compatible code/routes/schema aliases. This task is ACTIONABLE now because the naming decision is final; the only blocked part is any broad physical DB/route/event mass rename, which remains out of scope.

## Implementation contract
1. Inventory user-facing strings in 01-NPD UI/i18n/prototype references and task-owned docs that say Factory Article/FA where the user should see Finished Good/FG.
2. Add or update a central terminology mapping/helper if the codebase has i18n/labels, e.g. `legacy FA code -> FG Code (legacy FA code)` and `FA Projects -> FG Projects`.
3. Update EN and PL translations/user-facing labels for NPD screens, modals, empty states, buttons and validation messages so new UI says FG.
4. Preserve technical identifiers and compatibility routes unless a safe alias already exists. If routes stay `/npd/fa/[productCode]`, add breadcrumb/helper copy that displays FG and do not rename route files.
5. Update prototype/documentation red-lines if any implementation task reads prototype labels directly: `fa_code`, `NPD_FAS`, and old FA labels are compatibility aliases.
6. Ensure D365 copy says optional export/import integration and never implies D365 is source of truth.
7. Ensure post-release edit copy says new BOM/product-spec version + Technical approval, not in-place edit/built flag reset.

## Acceptance criteria
- Given NPD list/detail/create/brief-convert/builder screens render, when user-facing text is inspected, then canonical labels say FG/Finished Good rather than Factory Article/FA except explicit compatibility notes.
- Given a compatibility field is displayed, when labelled, then it is clear as `FG Code` or `FG Code (legacy FA code)` rather than canonical FA.
- Given existing routes/schema/events use `fa` identifiers, when tests run, then backwards compatibility remains and no DB/route/event mass rename occurred.
- Given D365 copy appears, when inspected, then it says export/import integration only and not source of truth.
- Given post-release edit copy appears, when inspected, then it requires new BOM/product-spec version and Technical approval before factory use.
- Given PL translations exist, when inspected, then they use FG / wyrób gotowy consistently and do not introduce FA as final term.

## Test strategy
- Snapshot/RTL tests for key NPD screens asserting FG labels.
- Grep guard for user-facing `Factory Article`/`FA Projects` strings outside allowlisted compatibility comments/tests.
- Route compatibility test proving existing `/npd/fa/...` still works if route rename is not performed.
- i18n smoke test for EN+PL keys.

## Risk red lines
- Do not rename physical DB tables/columns, route segments, event names or external integrations in this task unless they already have compatibility aliases and tests.
- Do not remove compatibility aliases/views.
- Do not rename external D365/customer item codes that contain FA/PR by business convention.
- Do not combine with T-092/T-093 BOM schema/backfill.
- Do not treat FA as final user-facing term; it is alias only.

pipeline_inputs.description: ACTIONABLE final-decision terminology pass: user-facing NPD labels use FG/Finished Good while preserving FA compatibility aliases and avoiding risky DB/route/event mass rename.
pipeline_inputs.details:
Terminology pass after gate/G3 tasks; UI/i18n/docs only, no DB/route/event mass rename.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- PL translations use FG / wyrób gotowy consistently.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

NPD prototype/documentation label notes where implementation tasks read prototypes

Existing fa routes/schema/events remain backward compatible; no mass rename.
D365 copy says export/import only and post-release edits require new BOM/spec version plus Technical approval.

Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: PL translations use FG / wyrób gotowy consistently.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

Grep guard for user-facing Factory Article/FA Projects outside allowlisted compatibility comments/tests.

i18n smoke test for EN+PL keys.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
